title: Must manually create directory `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` for buildah overlayfs to work on Alpine Linux 3.7
body:
**Description**

I recently ported `buildah` v0.11 to Alpine Linux and on an Alpine Linux 3.7 machine (bare metal x86_64). It will fail to use overlayfs unless you manually create `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` post install. 

I received the error, listed below, when attempting to use `buildah` which stated I did not have support for the overlay filesystem. I manually verified that the overlay kernel module was loaded and even manually created an overlay file system via `mount`.  After injecting debug code into the `github.com/containers/storage` dependency for the build I was able to tease out that there was an expectation of the directory `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay` being preset when it in fact was not. Simply running the following solves the issue:

```
sudo mkdir /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay
```

**NOTE:** I'm aware that `buildah` has no support for Alpine Linux at the moment but as this appears to be unrelated to the OS it seemed best to submit it as others may have a similar issue.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Build `buildah` v0.11, using the tar.gz from the release page of this repository, on Alpine Linux 3.7 with default Go compiler and dependencies as listed below.
2. Clean out `/var/lib/containers` if it is present from prior uses.
3. Run `sudo buildah --debug from scratch`

Build dependencies:
```
go=1.9.2-r1
btrfs-progs-dev=4.13.2-r0
device-mapper-libs=2.02.175-r0
lvm2-dev
gpgme-dev=1.9.0-r1
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ sudo buildah --debug from scratch
ERRO[0000] 'overlay' not found as a supported filesystem on this host. Please ensure kernel is new enough and has overlay support loaded. 
DEBU[0000] devicemapper: kernel dm driver version is 4.35.0 
ERRO[0000] devmapper: Udev sync is not supported. This will lead to data loss and unexpected behavior. Install a more recent version of libdevmapper or select a different storage driver. For more information, see https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/dockerd/#storage-driver-options 
DEBU[0000] [zfs] zfs command is not available: exec: "zfs": executable file not found in $PATH 
working-container
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
$ sudo buildah --debug from scratch
DEBU[0000] overlay test mount with multiple lowers succeeded 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false 
INFO[0000] [graphdriver] using prior storage driver: overlay 
working-container
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ sudo apk info buildah
buildah-0.11-r001 description:
A command line tool used to creating OCI Images

buildah-0.11-r001 webpage:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah

buildah-0.11-r001 installed size:
15794176
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:       0.11
Go Version:    go1.9.2
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    
Built:         Sat Feb 10 12:26:00 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
3.7.0
NAME="Alpine Linux"
ID=alpine
VERSION_ID=3.7.0
PRETTY_NAME="Alpine Linux v3.7"
HOME_URL="http://alpinelinux.org"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.alpinelinux.org"
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux mybox 4.9.65-1-hardened #2-Alpine SMP Mon Nov 27 15:36:10 GMT 2017 x86_64 Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
cat: can't open '/etc/containers/storage.conf': No such file or directory
```

comments.body:
Thanks for making this work on Alpine

/etc/containers/storage.conf is provided by skopeo-containers package.  This content should be shared between all products that use containers/storage, buildah, cri-o, podman, skopeo.  Has skopeo been packaged up for Alpine?

I don't understand why you would need to create /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay directory.  That should happen automatically.

@rhatdan I donãt understand either, maybe itãs because WIP packaging only installed `buildah` and none of the other packages. Anyways thatãs the code block I injected debug code into, Iãll test against master tomorrow to see if the issue has been fixed there (only have my tablet for today). And skepeo hasnãt been packaged yet, but my plan is to package the entire eco system. Iãd personally love to add a Gentoo build at some point in the future but am starting with Alpine for now. If you donãt mind, could you either post or point me to some documentation of all the things which should be packaged related to the `buildah` ecosystem? Currently Iãve got `buildah`, `runc`,  `skopeo`, and was also going to do `rkt`. Iãm personally interested in using Hashicorp Nomad as my container scheduler, instead of Kubernetes, but am happy to add a few other things to my Alpine package todo list. Iãll probably be a few weeks but I plan to submit them to Alpine to become official packages for the distro.

Awesome.  Here are the packages buildah requires on Fedora according to RPM.

```
rpm -q buildah --requires
container-selinux
libassuan.so.0()(64bit)
libc.so.6()(64bit)
libc.so.6(GLIBC_2.14)(64bit)
libc.so.6(GLIBC_2.2.5)(64bit)
libdevmapper.so.1.02()(64bit)
libdevmapper.so.1.02(Base)(64bit)
libdevmapper.so.1.02(DM_1_02_97)(64bit)
libgio-2.0.so.0()(64bit)
libglib-2.0.so.0()(64bit)
libgobject-2.0.so.0()(64bit)
libgpg-error.so.0()(64bit)
libgpgme.so.11()(64bit)
libgpgme.so.11(GPGME_1.0)(64bit)
libgpgme.so.11(GPGME_1.1)(64bit)
libostree-1.so.1()(64bit)
libostree-1.so.1(LIBOSTREE_2016.3)(64bit)
libostree-1.so.1(LIBOSTREE_2016.8)(64bit)
libpthread.so.0()(64bit)
libpthread.so.0(GLIBC_2.2.5)(64bit)
libpthread.so.0(GLIBC_2.3.2)(64bit)
libselinux.so.1()(64bit)
ostree
rpmlib(CompressedFileNames) <= 3.0.4-1
rpmlib(FileDigests) <= 4.6.0-1
rpmlib(PayloadFilesHavePrefix) <= 4.0-1
rtld(GNU_HASH)
runc >= 1.0.0-17
skopeo-containers >= 0.1.20-2
rpmlib(PayloadIsXz) <= 5.2-1
```
container-selinux is probably not required, since I don't think alpine supports SELinux.
skopeo-containers and runc are the two that you need content from.

If you package up these for alpine on any other platform and make them available for others, we should update the docs to mention them.
@code-ape Is this issue still open, or are you satisfied with the answer?
@rhatdan I think this is good to be closed. I got half way done with packaging and work has flared up the last couple weeks. Iãll submit a new issue when itãs done. Thanks for circling back!
